title: V-19 landspeeder
content:

Then get ready to run.|[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], briefing Gian speeder pilots before an attack on Trade Federation AATs|}} The '''V-19 landspeeder''', commonly referred to as a '''Gian speeder''', was a small [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] used by the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]], alongside the lighter [[Seraph-class urban landspeeder|flash speeder]]. ==Characteristics== SoroSuub had designed the V-19 Landspeeder to operate as both civilian and military transports. The vehicle's medium-grade hull plating and reinforced armor made it a common sight on congested urban worlds where collisions were commonplace. [[File:V-19_landspeeder.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A V-19 landspeeder.]] The V-19 was also equipped with thrust pods tucked in at its back to reduce target silhouette. This made it popular with the Naboo Royal Security Forces which deployed it for a range of situations including crowd control, wilderness excursions and urban battles. Although Naboo was a fairly peaceful planet, the Gian speeder was nonetheless rather heavily armed, with 2 forward light repeating blasters that could easily disable non-military vehicles and an optional medium [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] turret. Each blaster had its own dedicated auxiliary power unit which allowed it to operate even if the speeder's primary power generator was damaged. The removable medium blaster cannon was able to pierce the lower forward armor below the pilot's hatch of an AAT. Some Gian speeders didn't carry this cannon, their pilots favoring a larger windshield. Each carried a pilot, gunner and 2 passengers which were usually more than enough to handle a situation. Command versions of the vehicle featured holographic projection systems for tactical planning and more advanced communication systems. ==History== [[File:Gian.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Gian speeder used during the Second Battle of Theed.]] By the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], there were 36 Gian speeders in use by the RSF. When the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] launched its invasion of Naboo in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],''Battle for Naboo'' itself refers to its events taking place during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. a Gian speeder was used at a [[Naboo resistance base]].[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Gavyn Sykes/Legends|Gavyn Sykes]] drove another speeder at an [[Battle of the south ridge|engagement]] at the [[south ridge of Theed]]. During the invasion, Gian speeders were deployed to ambush lone AATs. This created a [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|diversion]] that allowed [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] and a small force to storm the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]].{{Film|I}} ==Behind the scenes== V-19 landspeeders first appeared in [[1999]] with the release of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]''. ==Appearances==
